title: index property non-root
description: Root object `{b: string; c: {...}}` where the nested `c` carries a declared `a` plus a string-valued index signature. JSON and binary round-trip the fixed root shape while the nested `c` admits arbitrary dynamic string keys alongside `a`.
notes: Only the nested `c` has an index signature, so its dynamic keys survive strip and preserve identically; the root has a fixed declared shape.
pureType: createJsonEncoder<{b: string; c: {a: string; [key: string]: string}}>(undefined, {strategy: 'clone'})
schema: createJsonEncoder(RT.object({b: RT.string(), c: RT.intersection(RT.record(RT.string()), RT.object({a: RT.string()}))}))
